channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:

item.title: Scientists find wind blowing from our Milky Way's black hole after half-century search: 'There it is' - Space
item.description:
1. Scientists find wind blowing from our Milky Way's black hole after half-century search: 'There it is'  Space
  2. Astronomers just solved a 50-year-old mystery about the Milky Way’s black hole  Scientific American
  3. Scientists have made a huge discovery at the enormous black hole at the centre of our Galaxy. And they found something pointing right at it  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  4. Wind from Milky Way's supermassive black hole is finally discovered  Reuters
  5. Even quiet black holes create winds, new Milky Way observations reveal  Science News

item.title: ‘They surprise me every time’: bees can use tools to solve problems, study finds - The Guardian
item.description:

item.title: The Cosmic Web Like You've Never Seen it Before - Universe Today
item.description:
1. The Cosmic Web Like You've Never Seen it Before  Universe Today
  2. Drilling down on dark matter  CERN Courier
  3. Questioning everything  Scientific American
  4. James Webb reveals the most detailed map of the universe's "invisible skeleton," connecting 164,000 galaxies from when the cosmos was about 1 billion years old and showing how the cosmic web influences the growth and end of star formation.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. James Webb uncovers dark matter shaping unseen cosmic web of the universe  starlust.org

  5. Cell study links Bathynomus supergiant deep-sea isopods to 5-year fasting survival  동아사이언스

item.title: Jupiter’s Great Red Spot is a storm wider than Earth that has raged for at least 150 years. Once large enough to swallow several Earths, it has been shrinking for more than a century, and astronomers can now track its changing size decade by decade. - Space Daily
item.description: Jupiter’s Great Red Spot is a storm wider than Earth that has raged for at least 150 years. Once large enough to swallow several Earths, it has been shrinking for more than a century, and astronomers can now track its changing size decade by decade.  Space Daily
